en: Then it was the poets gathered
ga: Ansin a chruinnigh na filí
speaker: narrator

en: Then indeed it was a sorrow
ga: Agus go deimhin bhrónach an scéal
speaker: narrator

en: For they found the Táin had perished
ga: Óir dáimsigh síad go raibh an Táin caillte
speaker: narrator

en: Where is it now?
ga: Cá bhfuil sé anois?
speaker: forgall

en: cried Forgall the Stammerer
ga: a diar Forgall Stadach
speaker: narrator

en: Where is our Raid of Cooley?
ga: Cá bhfuil ár Táin Cúailnge?
speaker: forgall

en: gone!
ga: imithe!
speaker: muirenn

en: said grey Muirenn
ga: arsa Muireann liath
speaker: narrator

en: Not one line fit for the telling
ga: Níl líne fágtha gur fiú an insint
speaker: muirenn

en: Then indeed it was a sorrow
ga: Ba mhór an t-uafás orthú
speaker: narrator

en: Twisted-Mouth Tigernach rose
ga: d'éirigh Tiarnach Cham Bhéil
speaker: narrator

en: shaking his rowan staff
ga: Ag chraitha maide chaorthainn
speaker: narrator

en: Listen to me
ga: Éist liom
speaker: tigernach

en: Listen to me though ye will not like it
ga: Éistigí liom cé nach dtaitneoidh sé libh
speaker: tigernach

en: I have read the Táin
ga: Tá an Táin léite agamsa
speaker: tigernach

en: In a book the yellow worms had eaten
ga: I téacs a d'ith na péiste buí
speaker: tigernach

en: In that telling, Cú Chulainn was not there
ga: Sa leagan sin, ní raibh Cú Chulainn ánn
speaker: tigernach

en: No Hound upon the border!
ga: Deabhail Cú ar an teorainn!
speaker: tigernach

en: Some thin-armed monk thrust him in later
ga: Sháigh manach caolghéagach éigin isteach ar ball é
speaker: tigernach

en: For the province of his patron
ga: Ar son cúige a oide
speaker: tigernach

en: Leinster's hand is in it brothers
ga: Tá lámh Laighean ann a bhráithre
speaker: tigernach

en: Leinster's hand has always stirred it!
ga: Muintir Laighean mar is de gnáth!
speaker: tigernach

en: Up leapt Blind Forgaill red-faced
ga: Léim Dallán Forgaill ina sheasamh an lasair ina ghruanna
speaker: narrator

en: Thou art lying through thy grey teeth!
ga: Tá tú ag insint bréag trí do chuid fiacla liatha!
speaker: forgall

en: The Hound was there upon the border
ga: Bhí an Cú ar an teorainn go deimhin
speaker: forgall

en: There he stood alone against armies
ga: Sheas sé leis féin in aghaidh sluaite
speaker: forgall

en: Show me then the old manuscript
ga: Taispeáin dom an seanscríbh mar sin
speaker: tigernach

en: Where his name is set before the Brown Bull's driving
ga: Áit a bhfuil a ainm curtha roimh ruathar an Donn
speaker: tigernach

en: Thou canst not. It is not there man
ga: Ní féidir leat. Ní ánn dó a mhic
speaker: tigernach

en: He was thrust in like a cuckoo
ga: Sáitheadh isteach mar cuach é
speaker: tigernach

en: Cuckoo Cuckoo!
ga: Cuach Cuach!
speaker: tigernach

en: Then the hall was all confusion
ga: Bhí an halla bun os cionn an uair sin
speaker: narrator

en: Then the hall was all uproar
ga: Le rírá agus rúille búille
speaker: narrator

en: Ulster's poets rose up shouting
ga: Sheas filí Uladh suas le béic
speaker: narrator

en: Munster's poets rose up answering
ga: Sheas filí na Mumhan le freaga
speaker: narrator

en: One man hit another sharply
ga: Bhuail fear amháin fear eile go docht
speaker: narrator

en: Tore the fine brooch from his shoulder
ga: Ag straceadh dealg bhreá dá ghualainn
speaker: narrator

en: Three harps fell and none would right them
ga: Fágadh trí chruit áit ar thuit síad
speaker: narrator

en: And still old Tigernach kept shouting
ga: Agus lean sean Tiarnach síar ag béicigh
speaker: narrator

en: Cuckoo! Cuckoo! He was planted!
ga: An chuach! An chuach! Sáithe isteach!
speaker: tigernach

en: Find the older text!
ga: Aimsigh an seantéacs!
speaker: tigernach

en: Ha! Text?
ga: Áh! Téacs an ea?
speaker: maebh

en: Hear me now ye thick-skulled lords
ga: Éistigí liom anois a thiarnaí thiubh
speaker: maebh

en: Who weigh a queen's worth by her husband's hoard
ga: A comhríonn fiúntas ríon i saibhreas rí
speaker: maebh

en: I'll not be reckoned so!
ga: Ní mheasfar mise mar sin!
speaker: maebh

en: Who speaks thus?
ga: Cé hí seo atá ag caint?
speaker: forgall

en: With voice that silences bards mid-quarrel?
ga: Le guth a chuireann tost ar filí i lár rac?
speaker: forgall

en: I am the lightning in the blood of Connacht
ga: Mise an splanc i cúisle na Chonnachta
speaker: maebh

en: I am the sword-gleam on the western water
ga: Mise lonradh lainne ar uiscí an iarthair
speaker: maebh

en: I am queen over Connacht's chieftains
ga: Banríonn thaoisaigh Chonnacht mé
speaker: maebh

en: as the storm is queen over the sea!
ga: Mar stoirm thar an bhfairraige mhóir!
speaker: maebh

en: My wars called men's quarrels
ga: Mo cogaí tuighta mar achrann na fir
speaker: maebh

en: My rule  unmade!
ga: Mo fhlaitheas scriosta!
speaker: maebh

en: I who stood astride the chariot reins in hand
ga: Mise a sheas ard sa charbaid úim sa lámh
speaker: maebh

en: I shall speak the truth
ga: Inseoidh mise an fhírinne
speaker: maebh

en: That every warrior-queen,
ga: Chun gach ríon chogaidh,
speaker: maebh

en: you buried out of sight be reclaimed
ga: A chuir sibh as radharc a tairtháil
speaker: maebh

en: O bright and fierce queen
ga: A bhanríon gheal fhiáin
speaker: forgall

en: What would you undo
ga: Cad a cheartfá
speaker: forgall

en: That learned men have twisted crooked?
ga: Atá stangtha ag insint na saoi?
speaker: forgall

en: The truth the Táin you kept from telling
ga: Fírinne an Táin a d'fhág sibh síar
speaker: maebh

en: is the tale of what a sovereignty costs
ga: Is ea, scéal an phraghais ar bhflaitheas
speaker: maebh

en: They told it thus:
ga: Seo mar a ninsíodh é:
speaker: maebh

en: Maeve had her wealth
ga: Bhí a saibhreas féin ag Meadhbh
speaker: maebh

en: had her cattle
ga: A cuid bó aici
speaker: maebh

en: had her gold
ga: Bhí ór aici
speaker: maebh

en: had great halls loud with feasting
ga: Bhí hallaí glóracha fleáúla aici
speaker: maebh

en: had all things but one thing
ga: Is gach uile ní ach aon ní amháin aici:
speaker: maebh

en: The Brown Bull of Cooley
ga: Tarbh Donn Cúailnge
speaker: maebh

en: That she must borrow or seize
ga: Nach mór di a iasacht nó a gabháil
speaker: maebh

en: to make her tally true.
ga: Go seasfadh a ríomh.
speaker: maebh

en: Ailill did boast his white-horned bull
ga: Mhaíomh Ailill a tharbh fionn-adharcach
speaker: maebh

en: And my bull was compared to his
ga: Agus cuireadh mo tharbh i gcomparáid leis
speaker: maebh

en: But my captains rode beside his
ga: Ach mharcaigh mo thaoiseach in aice a chuid-se
speaker: maebh

en: And my dreaming stood as tall as his
ga: Agus sheas mo thoil go cothrom leis
speaker: maebh

en: Hear me you who name me war-bringer
ga: Éistigí, sibhse a bhaist 'bean an chogaidh' orm
speaker: maebh

en: you who lay the slaughter at my door
ga: Sibhse a fhágann an t-ár ar mo thairseach
speaker: maebh

en: I knew sovereignty's worth
ga: Thuig mé fhlaitheas
speaker: maebh

en: No woman should lie sleeping
ga: Níor cheart d'aon bhean codladh
speaker: maebh

en: While a man beside her counts
ga: Agus fear in aice léi ag comhaireamh
speaker: maebh

en: What is hers as though his own
ga: An méid gur léi mar a chuid féin
speaker: maebh

en: In Cuailnge stood the great dark marvel
ga: I gCuailnge a sheas an t-iontas dorcha
speaker: maebh

en: I asked as queen I asked as equal
ga: D'iarr mé mar bhanríon, díarr mé mar chomhchéim
speaker: maebh

en: I named fair terms
ga: Thairg mé tearmaí chothrom
speaker: maebh

en: I did not stay to be corrected
ga: Níor fhan mé le go gceartófaí mé
speaker: maebh

en: In haste I called my armies to me
ga: Brostaigh mé mo shluaite chugam
speaker: maebh

en: I followed the old true road
ga: Lean mé an sean bhóthar
speaker: maebh

en: As well as any king might
ga: Chomh maith le rí ar bith
speaker: maebh

en: And Cú Chulainn was at the fording-place!
ga: Agus bhí Cú Chulainn ag an áth!
speaker: maebh

en: He stood there young and bright
ga: Sheas sé ansin óg agus glé
speaker: maebh

en: While Ulster lay in Macha's pangs
ga: Fhad is a bhí Uladh sínte faoi malacht Mhacha
speaker: maebh

en: I did not fear him
ga: Ní raibh eagla orm roimhe
speaker: maebh

en: I walked to him I spoke to him
ga: Shiúil mé chuige labhair mé leis
speaker: maebh

en: I offered terms as queen to warrior
ga: Thairg mé téarmaí ó banríon go ghaiscíoch
speaker: maebh

en: And used the tools that queens must use
ga: D'úsáid mé neart an banríon
speaker: maebh

en: Call it wisdom call it cunning
ga: Tabhair gliceas air nó gaois más mian
speaker: maebh

en: The host of Connacht crossed the border
ga: Thrasnaigh slua Chonnacht an teorainn
speaker: maebh

en: And I was she who brought them through
ga: Ba mise a thug cheannaireacht
speaker: maebh

en: My raid was the balancing of the world
ga: Meá na saolta mo tháin
speaker: maebh

en: When the Dun Bull found his strength;
ga: Nuair a fuair an Donn a neart
speaker: maebh

en: Then did I see my triumph turn
ga: Ansin a chonaic mé mo bua ag chasadh
speaker: maebh

en: He gored the White through flank and side
ga: Sáith sé an fionn tríd lár agus easna
speaker: maebh

en: They tore the hillside from the hill
ga: Bhain siad an talamh den sliabh
speaker: maebh

en: Drove rivers from their beds;
ga: Thiomáin siad aibhneacha as a gcursaí;
speaker: maebh

en: Then did the White Bull stagger back
ga: Ansin thit an Tarbh Bán ar gcúl
speaker: maebh

en: His red blood blackened the plain;
ga: Dhuibhigh an fhuil dhearg an mhá;
speaker: maebh

en: He fell to the the broken ground
ga: Thit sé ar an gcré dhubh bhriste
speaker: maebh

en: And did not rise again
ga: Agus níor éirigh sé arís
speaker: maebh

en: The Dun Bull stood above the slain
ga: Sheas an Donn os cionn an mhairbh
speaker: maebh

en: His dreadful roar was heard
ga: Chualathas a bhúir uafásach
speaker: maebh

en: Then wandered off and died alone
ga: Ansin d'imigh sé chun bás leis féin
speaker: maebh

en: They say I started all of it for a bull
ga: Deirtar gur mhaithe le tarbh a chúsaigh mé seo
speaker: maebh

en: Not for Donn Cuailnge's broad brown back
ga: Ní ar son dhroim leathan  Dhonn Chuailnge
speaker: maebh

en: Not for Ailill's white-horned pride
ga: Ní ar son uabhair adharcbháin Ailealla
speaker: maebh

en: But this:
ga: Ach seo:
speaker: maebh

en: A queen who will not reckon is no queen
ga: Ní banríon í gan a cóir
speaker: maebh

en: They will ask what Medb was truly
ga: Fiafróidh siad cearbh í Meadhbh í féin
speaker: maebh

en: Was she reckless was she ruinous?
ga: An raibh sí místuama an raibh sí creachach?
speaker: maebh

en: Answer them with plainness:
ga: Freagair iad go soiléir:
speaker: maebh

en: One woman stood on a hill
ga: Sheas bean amháin ar chnoc
speaker: maebh

en: And set the land trembling
ga: Is chuir an saol ar crith
speaker: maebh

en: every daughter of Ireland O!
ga: gach iníon in Éireann ó!
speaker: maebh

en: Call upon her yet and hear her
ga: Glaoigh uirthi fós agus éist léi
speaker: maebh

en: She was equal in her asking;
ga: Bhí sí comhionann ina éilimh;
speaker: maebh

en: She has not ceased her answering.
ga: Níor stop sí ag freagairt.
speaker: maebh
